description: Canada Tech Corp — Canada — Royalty — CONFIRMED (conduit leg 2: ultimate BO qualifies)
legal_notes: Conduit scenario, leg 2 of 2. See case_08a (Cyprus SPV, REJECTED). The ultimate beneficial owner is Canada Tech Corp, a Canadian operating company. Poland–Canada DTC (2012) applies; treaty royalty rate: 10%. MLI PPT YES but entity has full substance — existing anti-abuse provisions in treaty satisfied. Three BO conditions met: (i) receives royalties for own economic benefit; (ii) no contractual obligation to pass on to another party; (iii) genuine business activity in Canada. When the Polish payer applies look-through, Canada Tech Corp qualifies at 10% treaty rate. This case is evaluated as a standalone single-entity check — the link to the Cyprus conduit (case_08a) is documented in both files.

input.substance_notes: Canadian technology company, Toronto HQ. 320 employees. IP developed and owned in Canada — dedicated R&D department with 80 engineers. DEMPE functions: full development, enhancement, maintenance, protection and exploitation control performed in-house. No contractual obligation to pass royalties to any other party. Independent board of directors. Generates revenue from licensing IP to multiple unrelated parties globally, not exclusively from Polish group.

expected.eval_note: Conduit leg 2. Ultimate BO qualifies at 10% treaty rate. When read together with case_08a, the agent should have flagged conduit_risk on case_08a and prompted look-through to this entity.
